title: Lantern v0.1 — Phase 2a: multi-provider selector (choose provider + model at runtime)
intent: Let the user choose which OpenAI-compatible provider and model the chat uses at runtime — managed in Settings, persisted, and applied to every chat send — so Lantern can talk to any free provider (OpenRouter, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, Cerebras) or local Ollama without editing code. This is Phase 2a of v0.1; the real Notes/Tasks/Documents modules are a later brief (Phase 2b).
contextHandoff: Lantern is a self-hosted, local-first AI workspace; decisions are in .nogra/state/DECISIONS.md. Phase 1 (brief 587ac5) is built and verified locally on Windows: apps/web = Next.js 16.2.7 + React 19.2.4 + Tailwind v4 (App Router, TypeScript) with a persistent nav shell, a working streaming chat UI, and stub pages; apps/api = FastAPI with /health, an OpenAI-compatible streaming /chat (SSE), SQLite session persistence, and /sessions. Today the provider/base-url/model/key come only from environment variables. This phase adds Settings-managed provider configs persisted server-side and used by /chat. FIRST ACTION: read apps/web/AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md and .nogra/state/*; and because Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind v4 are newer than model training, read the relevant guides under apps/web/node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing any frontend code and heed deprecation notices. Build on the existing OpenAI-compatible client (no provider-specific SDKs). API keys must never be committed: store them only in the gitignored local data dir (e.g. apps/api SQLite under data/). Keep streaming provable with a stub/fake provider in tests — no live external calls, no secrets.
decisions: Provider/model selection lives in Settings and is persisted server-side (SQLite) so the backend /chat can resolve it; the active provider/model is applied on each chat request.
API keys are stored only in the local gitignored data dir, never committed; environment variables remain a default fallback.
Build on the existing OpenAI-compatible client layer; no provider-specific SDKs.
Chat must stay verifiable end-to-end against a stub provider in automated tests (no live calls, no secrets).
rejected: Full Phase 2 (provider selector + Notes + Tasks + Documents) in one run — too large to verify reliably; split into 2a (this) and 2b.
Storing provider API keys in the repo or a git-tracked config file — security no-go.
Per-message manual base-url/key entry in the composer — use a managed Settings list with an active selection instead.
knownGaps: The executor environment likely has no live provider key or running Ollama, so a real end-to-end external model call is a user action, not a run requirement. Selection + plumbing are proven via a stub provider plus persistence round-trip tests.

in: Backend: persist named provider configs in SQLite (label, baseUrl, model, apiKey) with an 'active' selection; GET/PUT/DELETE endpoints; store under the gitignored local data dir.
Backend: /chat resolves the active provider/model (or accepts providerId/model in the request body) instead of env-only; environment stays as the default fallback.
Backend: never return stored API keys in full — mask them on read; never write keys to a git-tracked file.
Web: Settings page UI to add/edit/delete provider configs and choose the active provider + model, persisted via the API.
Web: a provider/model indicator and quick switcher in the chat header that reads the active selection; chat send uses the active provider/model.
Web: client API helpers in apps/web/lib for the new settings/providers endpoints, with matching TypeScript types.

Tests: pytest for providers CRUD + active-selection persistence round-trip, /chat streaming using the selected provider/model against a STUB provider (no network/secrets), and API-key masking on read.

Docs/state: update .env.example and README provider-setup notes; update .nogra/state/SESSION-CHECKPOINT.md and CURRENT-TASKS.md (Phase 2a done; name Phase 2b = Notes/Tasks/Documents, Phase 3 = Memory/RAG).
out: Memory/RAG retrieval (Phase 3), Agent + tools, Deep Research, Compare, Email, Calendar, Cookbook/model-serving, image editor, PWA.
Calling live external AI providers in automated tests, or adding real API keys/secrets to the repo.
files: apps/api/main.py (EDIT — provider configs + active-selection persistence and endpoints; /chat uses active provider/model; key masking)
apps/api/test_main.py (EDIT — providers CRUD/persistence + stub-provider selection tests + masking)
apps/web/components/ (EDIT/NEW — provider switcher in chat header; settings form components)
.env.example (EDIT — document provider options; note keys live in local data, not committed)

successCriteria: In Settings, a user can add at least one provider config (label, base URL, model, API key), see it listed, edit and delete it, and mark one active; the active selection persists across reloads because it is served from the backend.
Stored API keys are never returned in full by the API (masked on read) and never written to a git-tracked file.

The chat header shows the active provider/model and lets the user switch it; sending a chat uses the selected provider/model — verifiable because the outbound request to the stubbed provider carries the selected base URL and model.

The backend exposes providers/settings CRUD + active-selection endpoints backed by SQLite in the gitignored data dir, and /chat resolves the active provider/model with environment as a default fallback.

Automated pytest covers providers CRUD + active persistence round-trip, /chat streaming using the selected provider/model against a stub provider (no network/secrets), and key masking — and all tests pass.
next build passes and the existing chat + session behavior still works.
.nogra/state/SESSION-CHECKPOINT.md and CURRENT-TASKS.md are updated to mark Phase 2a done and name Phase 2b (Notes/Tasks/Documents) and Phase 3 (Memory/RAG).
stopCriteria: First action: read apps/web/AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md and .nogra/state/*; then run pre-flight checks: `node --version`, `npm --version`, and `py -3.11 --version` (or `python --version`). If Node, npm, or Python 3.11+ is missing, status: blocked, name what is missing, and do not install runtimes or scaffold partial files.
Before writing any frontend code, read the relevant guides under apps/web/node_modules/next/dist/docs/ for Next.js 16 / React 19 / Tailwind v4 conventions; do not rely on pre-Next-15 assumptions.
Do not add real API keys or secrets to the repo and do not call live external providers, including in tests — use a stub/fake endpoint. Keys persist only in the gitignored local data dir.

Do not implement Phase 2b modules (real Notes/Tasks/Documents features), Memory/RAG, auth, or deployment; if delivering the selector would require going outside this slice, stop and return for a follow-up brief.
If `next build` or `pytest` exits non-zero and cannot proceed cleanly, stop with the command output rather than hand-patching a broken state.
Do not modify .nogra/ contracts or config.json; only update the named .nogra/state/* continuity files.

maxOutput.limit: no hard word limit; keep the opening summary concise and include all evidence needed to verify the result
